text: Norwegian Air has signed lease agreements with AerCap for 10 new Boeing 737-800 MAX 8 aircraft ahead of the European summer season of 2023, the airline said on Monday.

title: Emirates may cancel Boeing 777X if delays extend beyond 2023 -report
text: Dubai's Emirates could cancel an order for Boeing 777X passenger jets if the model's entry into service slips beyond the end of 2023, the airline's president was reported saying.

title: US FAA warns that 5G signals could interfere with landing of some Boeing 737s
text: The US Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) warned that 5G signals could interfere with the landing of some Boeing Co (NYSE:BA)'s 737 airplanes. The FAA issued an Airworthiness Directive revising the landing requirements for certain Boeing 737 planes at airports where 5G interference could occur.

title: FAA issues notice over Boeing 737s, 5G wireless interference
text: U.S. regulators have issued an airworthiness directive for most Boeing 737 airplanes over possible interference from 5G wireless broadband operations, according to a government notice published on Wednesday.

title: American Airlines (AAL) Cuts Summer Flights on Boeing's Delays
text: Boeing's Dreamliner aircraft delivery delays force American Airlines (AAL) to reduce international flying, including trimming Miami-Sao Paulo flights for this summer.

title: American Airlines cuts summer flights because of delayed Boeing jets
text: American Airlines is cutting its international summer schedule due to delayed deliveries of new jets.

title: American Airlines cuts summer flights due to Boeing 787 delays
text: American Airlines on Friday said it would cut more international flights over the summer than originally planned as it deals with delays in deliveries of the Boeing 787 Dreamliner long-haul jet.

title: American Airlines plans more schedule cuts as it waits for 787 jet deliveries
text: American Airlines Group Inc said on Friday it plans to further trim its summer schedule due to Boeing Co's delay in delivering 787 Dreamliner jets.

title: The Wall Street Journal: American Airlines cuts international schedule due to Boeing 787 delivery delays
text: American Airlines Group Inc. is further trimming its summer flying schedule due to Boeing Co.'s continuing delays in delivering new 787 Dreamliners, people familiar with the matter said.

title: Russia Tensions Soar as Shelling, War Rhetoric Increase; Investors Flee Stocks
text: U.S. sees imminent Russian attack on Ukraine as artillery fire in the eastern part of the country picks up. Stocks are headed for another weekly decline.

title: Norwegian Air CEO considers Airbus jets amid drawn-out Boeing litigation
text: Norwegian Air may order jets from Airbus in the future unless ongoing litigation with Boeing over previous aircraft cancellations is resolved in a timely fashion, the carrier's chief executive told Reuters on Friday.

title: FAA will not allow Boeing to certify 787 Dreamliners for flight until manufacturing process meets its standards
text: Shipments of the Dreamliner were halted last May over manufacturing and inspection issues and have not yet resumed.

title: Optimism In Textron Stock A Trigger For Boeing?
text: The shares of Textron observed a strong rally in 2021 largely due to a surge in the company's Aviation segment's order backlog, whereas the shares of Boeing (NYSE: BA) have remained almost level. Textron designs and manufactures civilian and military helicopters and business jets.

title: Next U.S. aviation safety chief will face Boeing, reform challenges
text: The next head of the U.S. Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) will face challenges overseeing Boeing Co and a series of reforms mandated by Congress in the wake of two fatal 737 MAX crashes.

title: FAA needs 'systemic fix' to Boeing 787 Dreamliner production issues
text: The head of the Federal Aviation Administration said Thursday the agency needs a "systemic fix" from Boeing to address 787 Dreamliner production issues but said a new agency directive would not be a "choke point" for deliveries.

title: Boeing And 3 Other Stocks Bought By Insiders
text: US stocks closed mixed on Wednesday following the release of minutes from the recent Federal Reserve meeting. Investors, meanwhile, focused on some notable insider trades.

title: FAA administrator who oversaw Boeing 737 MAX return resigning
text: WASHINGTON/SEATTLE (Reuters) -The head of the Federal Aviation Administration who oversaw the return of the Boeing 737 MAX after two fatal crashes grounded the plane and the agency's zero tolerance toward unruly passengers will resign on March 31, the agency confirmed late Wednesday.

title: Western Global Airlines Orders For Two Boeing 777 Freighters
text: Boeing Co (NYSE: BA) has received a firm order for two 777 Freighters from cargo operator Western Global Airlines. Financial terms not disclosed.

title: Western Global Airlines Purchases Two Boeing 777 Freighters
text: SEATTLE, Feb. 16, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Boeing [NYSE: BA] and Western Global Airlines today announced a firm order for two 777 Freighters, the first new-production freighters for the all-Boeing cargo operator based in Estero, Fla. The order was finalized in January 2022 and is currently listed as unidentified in Boeing's order backlog.

title: The U.S. FAA still doesn't trust Boeing's quality control
text: Boeing Co (NYSE: BA) can no longer self-certify its 787 Dreamliners for airworthiness, announced the U.S. FAA on Tuesday. The newly produced units will need a final inspection by the regulator before they can be delivered.

title: Why Boeing Flew Higher Today
text: The FAA sets its terms for Dreamliner deliveries to resume.

title: US regulator, not Boeing, to do final certification on new 787s
text: US air safety regulators said Tuesday they will perform final inspections on new Boeing 787 planes, retaining an authority normally granted to jet manufacturers.

title: U.S. lawmakers want review of FAA Boeing 737 MAX oversight
text: Two U.S. lawmakers asked the Transportation Department's Office of Inspector General to review the Federal Aviation Administration's (FAA) oversight over Boeing Co's actions surrounding the 737 MAX.

title: FAA will conduct final inspections on new Boeing 787 Dreamliners
text: The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) said Tuesday it will retain the authority to issue airworthiness certificates for all Boeing 787 Dreamliner aircraft and perform final inspections on newly produced 787s.

title: Boeing Bags Five-Year Contracts To Support Korea's Three Critical Defense Platforms
text: Boeing Co (NYSE: BA) has received three performance-based logistics (PBL) contracts from the Republic of Korea's Defense Acquisition Program Administration (DAPA). The financial terms were not disclosed.

title: Korea Selects Boeing to Support Readiness for Three Critical Defense Platforms
text: SEOUL, South Korea, Feb. 15, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Boeing [NYSE: BA] has received three performance based logistics (PBL) contracts from the Republic of Korea's Defense Acquisition Program Administration (DAPA) to manage sustainment of Republic of Korea Air Force (ROKAF) F-15K fighter jets and Airborne Early Warning & Control (AEW&C) aircraft, and Republic of Korea Army (ROKA) CH-47 Chinook helicopters. The five-year contracts will improve aircraft availability by using performance data to inform logistics and supply chain management activities.

title: Malaysia Airlines' parent to lease 25 737 MAX jets from Air Lease Corp
text: Air Lease Corp said on Monday it would lease 25 Boeing 737 MAX jets to Malaysia Airlines' parent to be delivered between early 2023 and 2026 through the lessor's orderbook with the manufacturer.

title: Boeing Reaches Record $2 Billion in E-Commerce Sales
text: DALLAS, Feb. 14, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Boeing [NYSE: BA] achieved an annual record for e-commerce parts sales last year with more than $2 billion in online orders. Fueled by investment in digital tools, Boeing Distribution Inc. (formerly Aviall) sold nearly 70,000 parts products through its e-commerce site to commercial and government customers, eclipsing pre-pandemic levels.

title: Boeing confident of working through any disruption to titanium supplies - exec
text: Boeing Co is confident of working through any potential disruption in titanium supplies due to its "very diverse" supply base, a senior executive at the U.S. manufacturer said on Monday.

title: Boeing Stock Climbs Higher, Holds Above Key Level: What's Next?
text: Boeing Co. BA shares were trading higher Thursday and bouncing back after being able to hold above a key support level. The stock could be ready to start an upward trend if it can continue to form higher lows and hold above the support level.

title: Boeing Wins Big With MAX And Freighters
text: Boeing had a good start to the year with 75 net orders.

title: CNBC Investing Club: We're paring a problem stock on strength, would buy at lower levels
text: As discussed earlier today in our Morning Meeting, we are right-sizing our Boeing position into the stock's rally.

title: U.S. judge dismisses two charges against former Boeing 737 MAX technical pilot
text: A U.S. judge on Tuesday dismissed two charges against a former chief technical pilot for Boeing Co accused of deceiving federal regulators evaluating the company's 737 MAX jet, but rejected a request to dismiss the other four counts.

title: Boeing deliveries slip to three-month low as Dreamliner problems weigh
text: Boeing Co said on Tuesday it delivered 32 aircraft in January, the lowest in three months, as its sidelined 787 Dreamliner program continued to weigh on the planemaker's ability to capitalize on a recovery in air travel.

title: Boeing Buys 2M Gallons Of Sustainable Aviation Fuel For Commercial Operations
text: Boeing Co (NYSE: BA) entered a supply agreement for two million gallons (7.5 million liters) of blended sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) with EPIC Fuels. Deal terms not disclosed.

title: Boeing's Big $35 Billion Win
text: Boeing's Big $35 Billion Win

title: Boeing Buys Two Million Gallons of Sustainable Aviation Fuel for its Commercial Operations
text: SEATTLE, Feb. 7, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Boeing [NYSE:BA] today announced a supply agreement for two million gallons (7.5 million liters) of blended sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) with EPIC Fuels to power its Commercial Airplanes operations in Washington state and South Carolina through 2022. The agreement is the largest announced SAF procurement by an airframer and further demonstrates Boeing's commitment to decarbonizing aviation.

title: Boeing Stock Has a New Problem About Market Share
text: Citigroup analyst Charles Armitage is worried that Boeing won't recapture lost market share from Airbus.

title: Boeing Has a New Problem. It's About Market Share, Says Citi.
text: Citigroup analyst Charles Armitage is worried that Boeing won't recapture lost market share from Airbus.

title: Notable Stock Buys: Boeing and NextEra Energy
text: Boeing director Steve Mollenkopf bought $100,000 of stock, while Jim Robo, NextEra Energy's outgoing CEO, snapped up $5 million of stock.

title: Boeing to Highlight Sustainability, Technology and Partnerships at Singapore Airshow 2022
text: SINGAPORE, Feb. 6, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- At the Singapore Airshow this month, the largest in Southeast Asia, Boeing (NYSE: BA) will showcase its industry-leading portfolio of commercial, defense and support services by highlighting advanced systems and capabilities focused on safe and sustainable aerospace. Boeing's presence at the show will include its newest fuel-efficient widebody jet, the 777X, along with the company's offerings in maritime patrol aircraft, advanced fighters and trainers, and autonomous systems.

title: Wall Street's top analysts are bullish on these five stocks amid the market's turbulence
text: TipRanks analyst ranking service pinpoints Wall Street's best-performing stocks, like Boeing and Roblox.

title: Is Boeing Stock A Buy Or Sell After Recent Earnings?
text: Boeing's Q4 2021 core net loss per share was wider than what sell-side analysts had expected, because of a substantial non-cash charge for the 787 program. But Boeing delivered positive free cash flow in Q4 2021, and the company is expected to be free cash flow positive as well in 2022.

title: Qatar is adding the Boeing 737 MAX to its fleet amid an ongoing dispute with Airbus that has 'destroyed' the relationship between the carrier and planemaker
text: Qatar's decision to purchase the MAX will increase its loyalty to Boeing, but will also further exacerbate its dispute with Airbus.

title: Boeing Books Huge Win
text: Qatar Airways has become the launch customer of Boeing's new freighter, the Boeing 777-8F. Qatar Airways has signed for up to 50 Boeing 777-8F, but the agreement contains conversions and options.

title: American Airlines amends firm Boeing 737 MAX orders to 30, delays delivery of 787-9 aircraft
text: American Airlines Group Inc. AAL, +2.19% disclosed Wednesday that it amended its firm orders for Boeing Co.'s BA, +4.05% 737 MAX 8 aircraft to 30, with 15 aircraft scheduled for delivery in 2023 and the rest to be delivered in 2024. The increase comes after the air carrier said it amended its purchase agreement dated February 2013, in which American converts purchase options to buy 23 additional 737 MAX 8 aircraft, and said it plans to convert options this year to buy seven more aircraft.

title: Why Boeing Shares Are Taking Off Today
text: Boeing Co (NYSE: BA) is trading higher Tuesday after the company launched its 777-8 Freighter and recently announced an order for up to 50 aircraft from Qatar Airways.  Qatar Airways will be the 777-8 Freighter launch customer.

title: Boeing Launches 777-8 Freighter
text: On Monday, Boeing Co. ( BA , Financial) launched the new 777-8 Freighter and expanded its market-leading 777X and freighter families of jetliners with an order for up to 50 aircraft from one of the world's largest cargo carriers, Qatar Airways.

title: Ethiopian Airlines to fly 737 MAX with passengers for first time since deadly crash
text: Ethiopian Airlines is due to resume flying the Boeing 737 MAX plane on Tuesday, but opinions are divided on the airline's first flight using the model since a crash nearly three years ago forced regulators to ground the fleet globally.

title: Ethiopian Airlines to resume 737 MAX flights after 2019 crash
text: Ethiopian Airlines is set Tuesday to operate the Boeing 737 MAX for the first time since a crash nearly three years ago killed all 157 people on board and triggered the global grounding of the aircraft.
